TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi sealed beforefieldinit SqlParameter extends System.Data.Common.DbParameter
Public NotInheritable Class SqlParameter Inherits DbParameter
public ref class SqlParameter sealed : System::Data::Common::DbParameter

Docs.summary: Represents a parameter to a and optionally its mapping to columns. This class cannot be inherited. For more information on parameters, see Configuring Parameters and Parameter Data Types.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Parameter names are not case sensitive.
> [!NOTE] > Nameless, also called ordinal, parameters are not supported by the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server.
For more information, along with additional sample code demonstrating how to use parameters, see [Commands and Parameters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/commands-and-parameters).
## Examples The following example creates multiple instances of <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> through the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameterCollection> collection within the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataAdapter>. These parameters are used to select data from the data source and put the data in the <xref:System.Data.DataSet>. This example assumes that a <xref:System.Data.DataSet> and a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataAdapter> have already been created by using the appropriate schema, commands, and connection. For more information and additional examples on using parameters, see [Retrieving and Modifying Data in ADO.NET](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/retrieving-and-modifying-data) and [Configuring Parameters and Parameter Data Types](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/configuring-parameters-and-parameter-data-types).
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/Classic WebData SqlParameter Example/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data/IDataParameter/Overview/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

Docs.param: The name of the parameter to map.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class that uses the parameter name and the data type.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The data type and, if appropriate, **Size** and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> are inferred from the value of the `dbType` parameter.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/Classic WebData SqlParameter.SqlParameter2 Example/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/.ctor/source2.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: The value supplied in the parameter is an invalid back-end data type.
Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public SqlParameter (string parameterName, object value);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(string parameterName, object value) cil managed
Public Sub New (parameterName As String, value As Object)
new System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter : string * obj -> System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter
public: SqlParameter(System::String ^ parameterName, System::Object ^ value);

Docs.param: The name of the parameter to map.
An that is the value of the .
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class that uses the parameter name and a value of the new .

remarks.format:
## Remarks When you specify an <xref:System.Object> in the `value` parameter, the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> is inferred from the Microsoft .NET type of the <xref:System.Object>.
Use caution when you use this overload of the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> constructor to specify integer parameter values. Because this overload takes a `value` of type <xref:System.Object>, you must convert the integral value to an <xref:System.Object> type when the value is zero, as the following C# example demonstrates.
```csharp Parameter = new SqlParameter("@pname", (object)0); ```
If you do not perform this conversion, the compiler assumes that you are trying to call the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.%23ctor(System.String,System.Data.SqlDbType)> constructor overload.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/Classic WebData SqlParameter.SqlParameter6 Example/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/.ctor/source5.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

Docs.param: The name of the parameter to map.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class that uses the parameter name, the , and the size.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The **Size** is inferred from the value of the `dbType` parameter if it is not explicitly set in the `size` parameter.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The **Size** is inferred from the value of the `dbType` parameter if it is not explicitly set in the `size` parameter.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Data.SqlTypes.SqlCompareOptions CompareInfo { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Data.SqlTypes.SqlCompareOptions CompareInfo
Public Property CompareInfo As SqlCompareOptions
member this.CompareInfo : System.Data.SqlTypes.SqlCompareOptions with get, set
public: property System::Data::SqlTypes::SqlCompareOptions CompareInfo { System::Data::SqlTypes::SqlCompareOptions get(); void set(System::Data::SqlTypes::SqlCompareOptions value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the object that defines how string comparisons should be performed for this parameter.
Docs.value: A object that defines string comparison for this parameter.

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.Data.DbType DbType { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Data.DbType DbType
Public Overrides Property DbType As DbType
member this.DbType : System.Data.DbType with get, set
public: virtual property System::Data::DbType DbType { System::Data::DbType get(); void set(System::Data::DbType value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the of the parameter.
Docs.value: One of the values. The default is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> are linked. Therefore, setting the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> changes the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> to a supporting <xref:System.Data.SqlDbType>.
For a list of the supported data types, see the appropriate <xref:System.Data.SqlDbType> member. For more information, see [DataAdapter Parameters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/dataadapter-parameters).
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/Classic WebData SqlParameter.IsNullable Example/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data/IDataParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.Data.ParameterDirection Direction { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Data.ParameterDirection Direction
Public Overrides Property Direction As ParameterDirection
member this.Direction : System.Data.ParameterDirection with get, set
public: virtual property System::Data::ParameterDirection Direction { System::Data::ParameterDirection get(); void set(System::Data::ParameterDirection value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the parameter is input-only, output-only, bidirectional, or a stored procedure return value parameter.
Docs.value: One of the values. The default is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks If the <xref:System.Data.ParameterDirection> is output, and execution of the associated <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand> does not return a value, the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> contains a null value.
`Output`, `InputOut`, and `ReturnValue` parameters returned by calling <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand.ExecuteReader*> cannot be accessed until you close the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader>.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
[Commands and Parameters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/commands-and-parameters)
[DataAdapter Parameters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/dataadapter-parameters)
[SQL Server and ADO.NET](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/sql/)

Docs.exception: The property was not set to one of the valid values.

MemberSignature.Value: public override bool IsNullable { get; set; }
.property instance bool IsNullable
Public Overrides Property IsNullable As Boolean
member this.IsNullable : bool with get, set
public: virtual property bool IsNullable { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the parameter accepts null values. is not used to validate the parameter's value and will not prevent sending or receiving a null value when executing a command.
Docs.value: if null values are accepted; otherwise, . The default is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks Null values are handled using the <xref:System.DBNull> class.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/DataWorks SqlParam.Properties/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet2":::

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

.property instance int32 LocaleId
Public Property LocaleId As Integer
member this.LocaleId : int with get, set
public: property int LocaleId { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the locale identifier that determines conventions and language for a particular region.
Docs.value: The locale identifier associated with the parameter.
Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public int Offset { get; set; }
.property instance int32 Offset
Public Property Offset As Integer
member this.Offset : int with get, set
public: property int Offset { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the offset to the property.
Docs.value: The offset to the . The default is 0.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The `Offset` property is used for client-side chunking of binary and string data. For example, in order to insert 10MB of text into a column on a server, a user might execute 10 parameterized inserts of 1MB chunks, shifting the value of `Offset` on each iteration by 1MB.
`Offset` specifies the number of bytes for binary types, and the number of characters for strings. The count for strings does not include the terminating character.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/DataWorks SqlParam.Properties/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet3"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet3":::

Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public override string ParameterName { get; set; }
.property instance string ParameterName
Public Overrides Property ParameterName As String
member this.ParameterName : string with get, set
public: virtual property System::String ^ ParameterName { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the name of the .
Docs.value: The name of the . The default is an empty string.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.ParameterName*> is specified in the form @paramname. You must set <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.ParameterName*> before executing a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand> that relies on parameters.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/Classic WebData SqlParameter.ParameterName Example/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data/IDataParameter/ParameterName/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public byte Precision { get; set; }
.property instance unsigned int8 Precision
Public Property Precision As Byte
member this.Precision : byte with get, set
public: property System::Byte Precision { System::Byte get(); void set(System::Byte value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of digits used to represent the property.
Docs.value: The maximum number of digits used to represent the property. The default value is 0. This indicates that the data provider sets the precision for .

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision> property is used by parameters that have a <xref:System.Data.SqlDbType> of `Decimal`.
You do not need to specify values for the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> properties for input parameters, as they can be inferred from the parameter value. `Precision` and `Scale` are required for output parameters and for scenarios where you need to specify complete metadata for a parameter without indicating a value, such as specifying a null value with a specific precision and scale.
> [!NOTE] > Use of this property to coerce data passed to the database is not supported. To round, truncate, or otherwise coerce data before passing it to the database, use the <xref:System.Math> class that is part of the `System` namespace prior to assigning a value to the parameter's `Value` property.
> [!NOTE] > Microsoft .NET Framework data providers that are included with the .NET Framework version 1.0 do not verify the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> or <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> of <xref:System.Decimal> parameter values. This can cause truncated data being inserted at the data source. If you are using .NET Framework version 1.0, validate the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> of <xref:System.Decimal> values before setting the parameter value. When you use .NET Framework version 1.1 or a later version, an exception is thrown if a <xref:System.Decimal> parameter value is set with an invalid <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*>. <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> values that exceed the <xref:System.Decimal> parameter scale are still truncated.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/Classic WebData SqlParameter.Precision Example/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/Precision/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public override void ResetDbType ();
.method public hidebysig virtual instance void ResetDbType() cil managed
Public Overrides Sub ResetDbType ()
override this.ResetDbType : unit -> unit
public: override void ResetDbType();

Docs.summary: Resets the type associated with this .

remarks.format: ## Remarks When executing a command that includes parameter values, code can either set the type of a parameter explicitly, or the parameter can infer its type from its value. Calling this method resets the parameter so that it can again infer its type from the value passed in the parameter. Calling this method affects both the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> properties of the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter>.

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public void ResetSqlDbType ();
.method public hidebysig instance void ResetSqlDbType() cil managed
Public Sub ResetSqlDbType ()
member this.ResetSqlDbType : unit -> unit
public: void ResetSqlDbType();

Docs.summary: Resets the type associated with this .

remarks.format: ## Remarks When executing a command that includes parameter values, code can either set the type of a parameter explicitly, or the parameter can infer its type from its value. Calling this method resets the parameter so that it can again infer its type from the value passed in the parameter. Calling this method affects both the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> properties of the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter>.

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public byte Scale { get; set; }
.property instance unsigned int8 Scale
Public Property Scale As Byte
member this.Scale : byte with get, set
public: property System::Byte Scale { System::Byte get(); void set(System::Byte value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the number of decimal places to which is resolved.
Docs.value: The number of decimal places to which is resolved. The default is 0.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale> property is used by parameters that have a <xref:System.Data.SqlDbType> of `Decimal`, `DateTime2`, `DateTimeOffset`, or `Time`.
> [!WARNING] > Data may be truncated if the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale> property is not explicitly specified and the data on the server does not fit in scale 0 (the default). > For the `DateTime2` type, scale 0 (the default) will be passed as datetime2(7). There is currently no way to send a parameter as datetime2(0). Scales 1-7 work as expected. > This problem applies to `DateTimeOffset` and `Time` as well.
You do not need to specify values for the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> properties for input parameters, as they can be inferred from the parameter value. `Precision` and `Scale` are required for output parameters and for scenarios where you need to specify complete metadata for a parameter without indicating a value, such as specifying a null value with a specific precision and scale.
> [!NOTE] > Use of this property to coerce data passed to the database is not supported. To round, truncate, or otherwise coerce data before passing it to the database, use the <xref:System.Math> class that is part of the `System` namespace prior to assigning a value to the parameter's `Value` property.
> [!NOTE] > .NET Framework data providers that are included with the .NET Framework version 1.0 do not verify the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> or <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> of <xref:System.Decimal> parameter values. This can cause truncated data to be inserted at the data source. If you are using .NET Framework version 1.0, validate the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Precision*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> of <xref:System.Decimal> values before setting the parameter value. <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Scale*> values that exceed the <xref:System.Decimal> parameter scale are still truncated.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/DataWorks SqlParam.Properties/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet4"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet4":::

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public override int Size { get; set; }
.property instance int32 Size
Public Overrides Property Size As Integer
member this.Size : int with get, set
public: virtual property int Size { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum size, in bytes, of the data within the column.
Docs.value: The maximum size, in bytes, of the data within the column. The default value is inferred from the parameter value.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Return values are not affected by this property; return parameters from stored procedures are always fixed-size integers.
For output parameters with a variable length type (nvarchar, for example), the size of the parameter defines the size of the buffer holding the output parameter. The output parameter can be truncated to a size specified with <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size*>. For character types, the size specified with <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size*> is in characters.
The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size> property is used for binary and string types. For parameters of type `SqlType.String`, `Size` means length in Unicode characters. For parameters of type `SqlType.Xml`, `Size` is ignored.
For nonstring data types and ANSI string data, the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size> property refers to the number of bytes. For Unicode string data, <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size*> refers to the number of characters. The count for strings does not include the terminating character.
For variable-length data types, <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size*> describes the maximum amount of data to transmit to the server. For example, for a Unicode string value, <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size*> could be used to limit the amount of data sent to the server to the first one hundred characters.
If not explicitly set, the size is inferred from the actual size of the specified parameter value.
If the fractional part of the parameter value is greater than the size, then the value will be truncated to match the size.
For fixed length data types, the value of <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Size*> is ignored. It can be retrieved for informational purposes, and returns the maximum amount of bytes the provider uses when transmitting the value of the parameter to the server.
For information about streaming, see [SqlClient Streaming Support](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/sqlclient-streaming-support).
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/DataWorks SqlParam.Properties/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet5"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet5":::

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public override string SourceColumn { get; set; }
.property instance string SourceColumn
Public Overrides Property SourceColumn As String
member this.SourceColumn : string with get, set
public: virtual property System::String ^ SourceColumn { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the name of the source column mapped to the and used for loading or returning the .
Docs.value: The name of the source column mapped to the . The default is an empty string.

remarks.format:
## Remarks When <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumn*> is set to anything other than an empty string, the value of the parameter is retrieved from the column with the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumn*> name. If <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Direction*> is set to `Input`, the value is taken from the <xref:System.Data.DataSet>. If <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Direction*> is set to `Output`, the value is taken from the data source. A <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Direction*> of `InputOutput` is a combination of both.
For more information about how to use the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumn> property, see [DataAdapter Parameters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/dataadapter-parameters) and [Updating Data Sources with DataAdapters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/updating-data-sources-with-dataadapters).
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/DataWorks SqlParam.Properties/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet6"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet6":::

Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public override bool SourceColumnNullMapping { get; set; }
.property instance bool SourceColumnNullMapping
Public Overrides Property SourceColumnNullMapping As Boolean
member this.SourceColumnNullMapping : bool with get, set
public: virtual property bool SourceColumnNullMapping { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the source column is nullable. This allows to correctly generate Update statements for nullable columns.
Docs.value: if the source column is nullable; if it is not.

remarks.format:
## Remarks <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumnNullMapping*> is used by the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommandBuilder> to correctly generate update commands when dealing with nullable columns. Generally, use of <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumnNullMapping*> is limited to developers inheriting from <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommandBuilder>.
<xref:System.Data.Common.DbCommandBuilder> uses this property to determine whether the source column is nullable, and sets this property to `true` if it is nullable, and `false` if it is not. When <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommandBuilder> is generating its Update statement, it examines the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumnNullMapping*> for each parameter. If the property is `true`, <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommandBuilder> generates a WHERE clauses like the following (in this query expression, "FieldName" represents the name of the field):
```tsql ((@IsNull_FieldName = 1 AND FieldName IS NULL) OR (FieldName = @Original_FieldName)) ```
If <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SourceColumnNullMapping*> for the field is false, <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommandBuilder> generates the following WHERE clause:
```tsql FieldName = @OriginalFieldName ```
In addition, @IsNull_FieldName contains 1 if the source field contains null, and 0 if it does not. This mechanism allows for a performance optimization in SQL Server, and provides for common code that works across multiple providers.

Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Data.SqlDbType SqlDbType { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Data.SqlDbType SqlDbType
Public Property SqlDbType As SqlDbType
member this.SqlDbType : System.Data.SqlDbType with get, set
public: property System::Data::SqlDbType SqlDbType { System::Data::SqlDbType get(); void set(System::Data::SqlDbType value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the of the parameter.
Docs.value: One of the values. The default is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> are linked. Therefore, setting the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> changes the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> to a supporting <xref:System.Data.SqlDbType>.
For a list of the supported data types, see the appropriate <xref:System.Data.SqlDbType> member. For more information, see [DataAdapter Parameters](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/dataadapter-parameters).
For information about streaming, see [SqlClient Streaming Support](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/sqlclient-streaming-support).

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public object SqlValue { get; set; }
.property instance object SqlValue
Public Property SqlValue As Object
member this.SqlValue : obj with get, set
public: property System::Object ^ SqlValue { System::Object ^ get(); void set(System::Object ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the value of the parameter as an SQL type.
Docs.value: An that is the value of the parameter, using SQL types. The default value is null.

remarks.format:
## Remarks For input parameters, the value is bound to the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand> that is sent to the server. For output and return value parameters, the value is set on completion of the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand> and after the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader> is closed.
This property can be set to null or <xref:System.DBNull.Value?displayProperty=nameWithType>. Use <xref:System.DBNull.Value?displayProperty=nameWithType> to send a NULL value as the value of the parameter. Use null or do not set <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlValue*?displayProperty=nameWithType> to use the default value for the parameter.
If the application specifies the database type, the bound value is converted to that type when the provider sends the data to the server. The provider tries to convert any type of value if it supports the <xref:System.IConvertible> interface. Conversion errors may result if the specified type is not compatible with the value.
Both the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> properties can be inferred by setting the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Value*?displayProperty=nameWithType>.
The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Value?displayProperty=nameWithType> property is overwritten by `SqlDataAdapter.UpdateCommand`.
Use the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Value?displayProperty=nameWithType> property to return parameter values as common language runtime (CLR) types.
For information about streaming, see [SqlClient Streaming Support](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/sqlclient-streaming-support).

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public override object Value { get; set; }
.property instance object Value
Public Overrides Property Value As Object
member this.Value : obj with get, set
public: virtual property System::Object ^ Value { System::Object ^ get(); void set(System::Object ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the value of the parameter.
Docs.value: An that is the value of the parameter. The default value is null.

remarks.format:
## Remarks For input parameters, the value is bound to the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand> that is sent to the server. For output and return value parameters, the value is set on completion of the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand> and after the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader> is closed.
This property can be set to `null` or <xref:System.DBNull.Value?displayProperty=nameWithType>. Use <xref:System.DBNull.Value?displayProperty=nameWithType> to send a NULL value as the value of the parameter. Use `null` or do not set <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Value*> to use the default value for the parameter.
An exception is thrown if non-Unicode XML data is passed as a string.
If the application specifies the database type, the bound value is converted to that type when the provider sends the data to the server. The provider tries to convert any type of value if it supports the <xref:System.IConvertible> interface. Conversion errors may result if the specified type is not compatible with the value.
Both the <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.DbType*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.SqlDbType*> properties can be inferred by setting the Value.
The <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.Value> property is overwritten by `SqlDataAdapter.UpdateCommand`.
For information about streaming, see [SqlClient Streaming Support](/dotnet/framework/data/adonet/sqlclient-streaming-support).
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter> and sets some of its properties.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_ADO.NET/DataWorks SqlParam.Properties/CS/source.cs" id="Snippet8"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Data.SqlClient/SqlParameter/IsNullable/source.vb" id="Snippet8":::

Docs.related: SQL Server Data Types and ADO.NET
Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public string XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase { get; set; }
.property instance string XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase
Public Property XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase As String
member this.XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase : string with get, set
public: property System::String ^ XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets the name of the database where the schema collection for this XML instance is located.
Docs.value: The name of the database where the schema collection for this XML instance is located.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This value is null (`Nothing` in Microsoft Visual Basic) if the collection is defined within the current database. It is also null if there is no schema collection, in which case <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.XmlSchemaCollectionName*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema*> are also null.

MemberSignature.Value: public string XmlSchemaCollectionName { get; set; }
.property instance string XmlSchemaCollectionName
Public Property XmlSchemaCollectionName As String
member this.XmlSchemaCollectionName : string with get, set
public: property System::String ^ XmlSchemaCollectionName { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets the name of the schema collection for this XML instance.
Docs.value: The name of the schema collection for this XML instance.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This value is null (`Nothing` in Microsoft Visual Basic) if there is no associated schema collection. If the value is null, then <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema*> are also null.

Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server

MemberSignature.Value: public string XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema { get; set; }
.property instance string XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema
Public Property XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema As String
member this.XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema : string with get, set
public: property System::String ^ XmlSchemaCollectionOwningSchema { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };

Docs.summary: The owning relational schema where the schema collection for this XML instance is located.
Docs.value: The owning relational schema for this XML instance.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This value is null (`Nothing` in Microsoft Visual Basic) if the collection is defined within the current database. It is also null if there is no schema collection, in which case <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.XmlSchemaCollectionDatabase*> and <xref:System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter.XmlSchemaCollectionName*> are also null.

Docs.related: Using the .NET Framework Data Provider for SQL Server
